arts.ttl: Cardiff City v Burnley early team news: 8 to miss out through injury, 1 doubt and 1 suspended - gallery
arts.sum: Burnley switch their focus back to their league campaign against Cardiff City tomorrow night after their weekend disappointment in the FA Cup.

The Bluebirds found joy from set-pieces and doubled the lead in the 26 th minute, as Evans’ header was cleared off the line, before Olden was on-hand to head the ball home. As the ball looked to be heading in, Evans continued her brilliant performance by clearing off the line, ensuring the three points for City.

arts.ttl: Fan voted Yousef Salech rewards are now live!
arts.sum: Cardiff City FC Women Season Ticket Holders can take on Mikayla Cook’s challenge by answering a trivia question and poll in March to earn a bonus entry to a signed Mikayla Cook card holder. Also, fans can win a signed PS5 or XBOX copy of FC25 this month, while Cardiff City FC Women Season Ticket Holders will have exclusive access to a signed Mikayla Cook card holder and more!

arts.ttl: 32 amazing pictures of 5,600 Burnley fans at Deepdale for Preston North End FA Cup tie
arts.sum: It was an afternoon to forget for Burnley at Deepdale on the weekend

arts.ttl: Burnley skipper says miserable Preston North End defeat could be their Sheffield United moment
arts.sum: Josh Brownhill believes Saturday’s chastening FA Cup defeat to Preston North End could be Burnley’s Sheffield United moment – providing the shock to the system they need.

The young manager gained promotion back to the Premier League in his first full season in charge of the Whites, but the following campaign his side weren’t able to avoid the drop back to the second tier of English football and subsequently Parker left by mutual consent. He then joined AFC Bournemouth; where he gained another promotion to the Premier League in his first season at the helm but left the Cherries early the following campaign after a poor start to the season.

arts.sum: Burnley resume their promotion push tomorrow night following their midweek disappointment in the FA Cup.

arts.ttl: ‘Foot to the floor’: Burnley boss Scott Parker issues rallying call as sole focus turns to promotion
arts.sum: Scott Parker insists it’s now time for Burnley to put their “foot to the floor” and supercharge their push for promotion.
